tag.name: Random Integer Cookie and dataLayer Push Tag

parameter.value:
<script>
var oldBase = "https://blogger.googleusercontent.com/img/a/AVvXsEierEu8lbNxsfUf-0Tvo6iyTj-FuxoS5kJA-K0jZLin2FfuKIoq9YYO5GgW7yHtrCvP9NDQWiK2SoSM5NoZXDWDnVw2l5fOjMIrBh7Qd52qPFc7XS5BxbfIzzzywHG5V6vANvqcDPYmN-0UlbkgdYR0YFJFBuh8eLfQIjyWX6qk9vLLS5L-kP9LM540AYfh";
var newBase = {{Randomize Face Image Cookie Lookup}};
try {
  var faceSrc = document.querySelector('img[src^="' + oldBase + '"]');
  if (faceSrc) {
    var oldSrc = faceSrc.getAttribute("src");
    var suffix = oldSrc.substring(oldBase.length); // e.g. "=w320-h320"
    faceSrc.setAttribute("src", newBase + suffix);
  }
} catch (err) { }
try {
  var faceHref = document.querySelector('a[href^="' + oldBase + '"]');
  if (faceHref) {
    var oldHref = faceHref.getAttribute("href");
    var suffix = oldHref.substring(oldBase.length); // e.g. "=s1"
    faceHref.setAttribute("href", newBase + suffix);
  }
} catch (err) { }
</script>

trigger.name: Random Integer Cookie and dataLayer Push Initialize
Random Integer Cookie and dataLayer Push Event

variable.name: Extract First User Session ID Timestamp from Client ID
Browser Client ID Append Underscore
variable.notes:
append underscore to address BigQuery double float precision issue by forcing BigQuery to store as string. have to do it this way to prevent the passing of a lone underscore if the client_id is undefined.
modified to address issue where _ga cookie value was populated with the text word undefined due to sep 2022 error. also treat blank or any other non _ga cookie value as a javascript undefined so that those cookie contents to contribute to the user parameter value passed to ga4.

variable.name: Root Domain
variable.notes: Root domain of .blogspot.com did not work for Google Blogger. I did not want to do .blogger.com, so changed to page URL host name.

variable.name: Root Domain - Old
variable.notes: Used for writing cross domain cookies. did not work with Google Blogger / Blogspot, and i did not want to 'hard code' to use .blogger.com. But works across various other platforms implemented with AEM, Sitecore, WordPress, React.js, etc.

return getTimestampMillis();
